AN ACT authorizing the Commonwealth of Kentucky to issue bonds

purpose of funding certain indebtedness of the State .

gregating more than fivehundred thousand dollars, which
debts have arisen because of certain deficits and failures in

the State ; and
Whereas ,It is to the interest of the State that at least

No. 1 .
RESOLUTION providing for a special committee to investigate the
Eddyville Penitentiary .
Whereas the Public Press of Kentucky has been filled
with statements referringto said management and lack of
disciplinein the branch penitentiaryat Eddyvilleand as this
legislaturemust determine itsfuture continuance as a peni-
tentiaryitis proper that we have allthe information that
willenable us to act forthe best interestof Kentucky ,there-
fore ,
Be itresolvedby theGeneralAssembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That there is hereby constituted and appointed a
specialcommitee of six, three from the Senate, and three
from the House of Representatives,to be composed of mem-
bers of the Committees on Penitentiary and House of Re-
form in the Senate and House respectively. The members
thereof to be selectedby the respectivechairman of said
committees . From the Senate Committee there shall be
selectedtwo Democrats and one Republican , and from the
House Committee there shall be selectedtwo Republicans
and one Democrat . It shall be the duty of said Special
Committee to examine into the discipline and conduct of
the penitentiaryat Eddyville,in order that the General As-
sembly may have an opportunity to consider whether it is
best to place the prisons in charge of a commission of busi-
ness men , with full power to remove any officersunder
them at pleasure .
2. Resolved ,Said committee are empowered to send for

personsand papers,swear witnessesreportingitswork to
thisHouse at as early a date as possible.
3. Resolved , That the Auditor of Public Accounts be
and he is hereby instructed to draw his warrant on the
Treasurer to pay the necessary expenses of said committee .
Became a law May 11,1897,without the approval of the Governor .
